description_long: This is a dark blue, single-propeller military airplane with a weathered, metallic exterior and yellow accents on the propeller tips and tail. The wings are broad and slightly angled, featuring white star insignias typical of World War II-era U.S. Navy aircraft. The fuselage is streamlined, with a bubble canopy cockpit positioned just ahead of the wing root, and the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers with yellow tips. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the engine cowling is rounded and robust. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of classic carrier-based fighter planes.
description: A weathered, dark blue single-propeller military airplane with yellow accents, broad wings, and a bubble canopy cockpit. White star insignias and a streamlined fuselage evoke a classic World War II fighter.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rounded nose, three-blade propeller with yellow tips, and the broad, slightly angled wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the bubble canopy cockpit, star insignia, and the streamlined fuselage with a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Top view reveals the full wingspan, yellow-tipped tail and wings, and the overall planform of the aircraft.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the cockpit, insignia, and tail configuration, with propeller and engine cowling visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral and no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable taildragger configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed bubble canopy cockpit, likely single-seat.
